behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local parsing/segmentation logic on a command string. Behavior text is about statement splitting only, with no required module handoff, filesystem, subprocess, network, or model output. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not require real shell execution or cross-component persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the segmenter with multiline inputs (assignments + command lines), asserting returned segment list preserves statement boundaries and ordering.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module interaction: segmentation output must feed trigger classification correctly. Unit tests of matcher alone can miss handoff bugs like the incident (`not_a_trigger` on whole block). Integration-brake checks: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency, no AI/ML output dependency, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring segmenter + trigger detector with the full multiline block; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is also boundary behavior between multiline segmentation and trigger intent classification; false-positive risk appears when modules compose. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text only concerns command classification logic, with no real infra or model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with multiline assignments + `gh issue create`; run through segmentation+classification pipeline and assert non-PR trigger outcome.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Needs multiple local components: trigger result, PR-id reconstruction logic, and state persistence layer. A single-function unit test can miss serialization/contract errors between these modules. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real `gh` subprocess or remote API; fixture inputs can drive reconstruction path.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test from detected PR-create event through PR-id extraction and state writer, then verify persisted state contains expected PR identifier fields.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: This is a producer-consumer contract across hooks via shared state file/schema. Real failure is cross-module handoff (writer -> reader/enforcer), not a single local function. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require OS-specific event semantics, external network/subprocess, or LLM-dependent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that runs post-tool state write, then invokes pre-tool enforcement reader against that written state; assert enforcement activates for matching PR context.
